nodes.label: Data Handling
nodes.description: Collecting, organising, and showing data using tables, pictographs, and bar graphs

nodes.label: Collecting Data
nodes.description: Gathering information by counting, observing, or doing a survey

nodes.label: Surveys
nodes.description: Ask people questions and record their answers one by one

nodes.label: Observation
nodes.description: Watch and count things happening, like vehicles on a road or birds in a park

nodes.label: Organising Data
nodes.description: Sorting collected data into groups using tally marks and tables

nodes.label: Tally Marks
nodes.description: Short lines to count: draw 4 lines, cross with a 5th to make a bundle of 5

nodes.label: Frequency Table
nodes.description: A table with categories, tally marks, and totals to organise data

nodes.label: Displaying Data
nodes.description: Showing data visually using pictographs and bar graphs

nodes.label: Pictographs
nodes.description: Charts using pictures; each picture stands for a number shown in the key

nodes.label: Bar Graphs
nodes.description: Charts using bars of different heights; read values from the scale on the axis

nodes.label: Reading Data
nodes.description: Interpreting charts to answer questions about the data

nodes.label: Most and Least
nodes.description: Tallest bar or most pictures = most; shortest bar or fewest pictures = least

nodes.label: Total and Difference
nodes.description: Add all values for total; subtract two values for difference

nodes.label: Creating Charts
nodes.description: Steps to make your own pictograph or bar graph

nodes.label: Step-by-Step
nodes.description: 1. Collect data 2. Organise with tallies 3. Choose chart type 4. Draw with title, labels, key/scale

nodes.label: Tips
nodes.description: Always add a title; use equal spacing for bars; choose a key that fits your numbers
